title: Screenshot 1: AWS Lambda "Create function" page with tutorial showing
content:
# Screenshot 1: AWS Lambda "Create function" page with tutorial showing

- Tutorial popup visible: "Task 1: Create the function" with Step 1/5
- Three options displayed: Author from scratch, Use a blueprint, Container image
- Selected "Use a blueprint" option
Screenshot 2: Blueprint configuration
- Blueprint selected: "Hello world function" with nodejs22.x
- Function name entered: mySimpleWebAppFunction
- Runtime: nodejs22.x, Architecture: x86_64
- Execution role: Create a new role with basic Lambda permissions
Screenshot 3: Lambda function code preview
- Preconfigured Hello World code shown in editor
- Code logs event keys and demonstrates basic Lambda structure
Step 1: Create Lambda Function
AWS Lambda Simple Web App Tutorial
Tutorial Overview
This tutorial demonstrates how to create a simple serverless web application using AWS Lambda, API Gateway, and DynamoDB.
GitHub Repository
Steps Taken:
/image
1. Selected "Use a blueprint" option
2. Chose "Hello world function" blueprint with Node.js 22.x runtime
3. Named the function: mySimpleWebAppFunction
4. Kept default execution role: Create a new role with basic Lambda permissions
5. Architecture: x86_64
6. Clicked "Create function”
Result:
✅ Function successfully created!
Function ARN: arn:aws:lambda:us-east-2:861870144562:function:mySimpleWebAppFunction
Execution role: mySimpleWebAppFunction-role-xbj15t0r
Permissions: Upload logs to Amazon CloudWatch Logs
Task 2: Invoke the Function
Next Tutorial: Create a Simple Web App
Tutorial Overview:
This is a more advanced tutorial that builds upon the Hello World function. It teaches how to:
• Build a simple web app with a Lambda function
• Create a function URL that outputs a webpage
• Invoke the function through its public function URL
• Deploy a real web application accessible via the internet
Screenshot 4: Tutorial Panel - Create a Simple Web App
- Tutorial panel shows next steps for web app creation
- Includes 'Start tutorial' button
- Tutorial focuses on function URLs for web output
Tutorial Steps Completed:
Task 1: Create the Function (5 steps) ✅
1. Selected "Use a blueprint" option
2. Chose "Getting started with Lambda HTTP" blueprint (nodejs22.x)
3. Named function: http-function-url-tutorial
4. Acknowledged public Function URL security statement
5. Created function successfully
Screenshot 5: Function Overview Page
- Function successfully created: http-function-url-tutorial
- Function URL generated and displayed
- Description: "Learn about Lambda by creating an HTTP endpoint that outputs a server-side rendered web page”
- Task 1 marked complete with green checkmark
Screenshot 6: Code Tab - index.mjs File
- Code editor showing the Lambda function implementation
- File structure visible: index.html and index.mjs
- Code imports Node.js fs module and reads HTML file
Screenshot 7: Complete Function Code
- Full handler function visible
- Returns HTTP response with status 200, Content-Type: text/html, and HTML body
- Deploy and Test buttons visible in sidebar
Task 2: Invoke the Function (2 steps) ✅
1. Reviewed the function code in the Code tab
2. Clicked the Function URL to invoke the web app
Screenshot 8: Live Web Application Result! 🎉
- Page title: "Hello from AWS Lambda!”
- Subtitle: "The blueprint function you created rendered this page”
- Tutorial content showing next steps to make the page dynamic
- Links to additional Lambda examples and use cases
- Public URL: https://fptqigvehwhqkqfkikmzhfsu3y0tumhs.lambda-url.us-east-2.on.aws/
